# Smudge reveals a sealed blob to plaintext on checkout; sesam clean removes
# that plaintext once the sealed object is dropped from the repository.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Seal the README created by sesam init; this writes the encrypted object to
# .sesam/objects/ and leaves the plaintext in place.
exec sesam seal
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'seal readme'

# Smudge: re-checking out the sealed object must reveal the plaintext.
exec git checkout HEAD -- .sesam/objects/README.md.sesam
exec cat README.md
stdout 'sesam bootstrap'

# Remove the sealed object from git - the secret is gone from the tree.
exec git rm .sesam/objects/README.md.sesam
exec git commit -m 'drop secret'

# Git does not invoke smudge for deleted objects; README.md lingers as stale
# revealed plaintext.
exists README.md

# sesam clean must wipe every untracked file left behind by a previous smudge.
exec sesam clean
! exists README.md
